content.claim: Across OECD countries, autism spectrum disorder diagnoses have increased two to fourfold over 15 years, with OECD evidence indicating this rise is largely attributable to improved detection and diagnostic practice rather than increased underlying autism prevalence
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Finding specific to autism, not generalizable to all SEN categories
Detection versus prevalence distinction cannot be conclusively resolved from diagnostic data alone
Cross-national comparisons complicated by varying diagnostic criteria and health system structures
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
